title: Trochera barbinodis
taxonomy: Trochera barbinodis is a species of plant in the family Zingiberaceae. This family consists of over 1,500 species of flowering plants, commonly known as gingers. The Zingiberaceae family is part of the order Zingiberales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. The Zingiberales order is further classified under the subclass Zingiberidae and the class Liliopsida. These classifications group plants based on their shared characteristics, such as leaf and flower morphology.
